paper_title: Study region, sampling, and laboratory protocols

methods.description: This method involves the collection and laboratory analysis of water samples from streams in the study region to measure various chemical properties and concentrations.

steps.description: Water temperature was measured in the field.
steps.searchable_summary: measure water temperature in the field

steps.description: In the laboratory, water samples were analysed for pH, water colour, conductivity, and concentrations of dissolved organic carbon (DOC), inorganic monomeric Al (Al im), organic monomeric Al, and SiO2.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze water samples for pH, color, conductivity, DOC, Al, and SiO2 concentrations

methods.description: This method describes the collection, preparation, and identification of diatom samples from various substrates to assess their ecological preferences and classify them based on acid sensitivity.

steps.description: Diatoms were collected from all available substrates (i.e., stones, macrophytes, and sediments) in each locality.
steps.searchable_summary: collect diatoms from stones, macrophytes, and sediments

steps.description: Diatoms were digested with acids in the laboratory, mounted in permanent slides, and identified to species in 300 frustule counts.
steps.searchable_summary: digest diatoms with acids, mount on slides, and identify species

steps.description: Species were classified as acid-sensitive and acid-tolerant based on ecological preferences.
steps.searchable_summary: classify diatom species based on acid sensitivity
